121.content:
The rain was coming down hard as an afternoon storm moved through the area. Having lived in the area my entire life, you’d think I would know better than to not have an umbrella in my car. But I didn’t, and I was thoroughly soaked by the time I made it inside my apartment building.

Today had been a rather slow day for me: processing some low-level street dealers while awaiting news on a case. Hector Chavez, the portly leader of the Chavez Cartel, was on trial for bribing City officials. Placing my things on the table just inside the door, I was on my way into the kitchen for a drink when I heard my cellphone ringing.

Returning to grab my phone, I saw that it was my brother calling. He wasn’t really my brother; Darren Acton and I spent our teenage years in the same foster home, along with our other brother Andrew Pierce. Answering the call, I said, “Hey Darren, what’s up?”

The tone of Darren’s voice told me he wasn’t happy, “Matt, you need to turn on the TV.”

“Why?” walking over to my TV, I said, “What’s going on?”

Turning on my TV and switching to the local news, I watched as a Breaking News alert flashed across the screen. Hector Chavez Innocent of All Charges. I couldn’t even listen to the reporters talking about it, that’s how angry I was. After a minute, I said, “They’re really letting that bastard go? After the months of work we put into bringing him down, all the evidence we gathered, they’re just letting him go?” covering my mouth for a moment, I said, “That bastard’s goons put Andy in the hospital for almost a month.”

“Trust me, I know exactly how you feel.” Pausing, Darren said, “Don’t worry; somehow we’ll find a way to put him behind bars for good.”

Looking at the TV again, I said, “First thing tomorrow we need to come up with a plan to make that bastard pay.”

Darren hung up shortly after, leaving me standing in my living room fuming with anger. Watching as the special report went to commercial, I said to myself, I’m going to make sure that bastard goes away; I don’t care what it takes.

********************************


I tried my best to not think about the news for the rest of the night, but it wasn’t easy. I was still pretty upset as I drove into work the next morning. After parking my car in the underground garage, I took an elevator upstairs.

I barely had a chance to sit at my desk before Darren and Andrew were standing in front of me. Looking at them, I saw a peculiar look on each of their faces. “What’s going on?”

“The boss wants to see us.” Looking at me, Andrew said, “I think it’s something to do with the Chavez case.”

“Okay, good. That bastard needs to be locked away.” Getting up from my chair, I followed them through the office to the conference room. Once inside I saw the head of the Narcotics Unit, Captain Howard Burnett, sitting at the table with an unfamiliar woman. As the three of us took our seats, I said, “Hey Cap; what’s going on?”

“By now I’m sure you’ve heard the news about Hector Chaves’ trial.” Sighing, Captain Burnett continued, “The Chief is furious to say the least; he wants us to create an airtight case to bring that bastard to justice.”

“Forgive me sir, but we did have an airtight case.” Looking at me and Andrew, Darren said, “We spent months gathering evidence to put him away; need I remind you that my brother was put in the hospital.”

“And where did all that work get us?” looking at us, Captain Burnett said, “I’ve been ordered to send one of you undercover to infiltrate the cartel; gathering information from the inside to take them down.”

I couldn’t help but chuckle at the idea, “I’m sorry sir; but Hector Chavez doesn’t trust whites. Since we’re all white, how are we going to get in?”

“That’s where this woman comes in.” Gesturing to the woman next to him, who’d remained silent the entire time, Captain Burnett said, “Gentlemen, allow me to introduce Loren Stewart of Radeon Scientific.”

After exchanging pleasantries, Ms. Stewart said, “Gentlemen, my company has developed technology which can change everything about a person.” Focusing on me, she said, “To that end; that’s why we’re going to transform Mr. Collins here into a 22-year-old Mexican woman.”
121.title: The Story Begins

1211.content:
My brothers and I all erupted in uproarious laughter once we heard that. I honestly thought Ms. Stewart was pulling my leg. Once the laughter started dying down, I could see the serious looks on Captain Burnett and Ms. Stewart faces. Roughly elbowing Andrew and Darren in their stomachs, I cleared my throat and said, “Wait, you’re serious?”

“That’s right.” looking at me, Ms. Stewart said, “My company, working in conjunction with your unit, will transform you into a Mexican national for the purpose of embedding you in the Chavez Cartel.”

“Okay that part makes sense.” While Darren and Andrew kept snickering, I said, “But why do I have to be a woman in order to do that?”

Focusing on me, Captain Burnett said, “I hate to say this; but he’s more likely to trust an attractive woman then some man he knows nothing about.”

My mind was going a mile a minute as I thought about everything I was being told. “Just so we’re clear; what exactly am I supposed to do once I’m embedded with the cartel?” looking incredulous, I said, “Because I’ll tell you right now; there’s no way I’m going to seduce Hector Chavez. The man’s in his fifties.”

While Darren and Andrew were once again laughing at the idea of me seducing someone, Captain Burnett said, “You won’t be seducing Hector Chavez; you’ll be seducing his son Miguel.”

While that made me feel a little better, I still wasn’t thrilled. Miguel Chavez was Hector’s 23-year-old son; rumored to be next in line to lead the cartel. “There’s one thing no one seems to be thinking of: I’m not gay. How does anyone expect me to seduce someone that I’m not physically attracted to?”

“The procedure we’ll put you through is quite extensive; you’ll become a woman both physically and mentally.” Looking at me, Ms. Stewart said, “And you needn’t worry; everything is 100% irreversible. Once your assignment is complete, you’ll be your old self again.”

Darren and Andrew had finally stopped laughing at that point. Composing himself, Darren said, “Just curious; but where do Andrew and I come into this?” pausing, looking concerned, he said, “We’re not going to become woman too, are we?”

“No; your brother will be the only one whose changed.” Looking at the three of us, Captain Burnett said, “You two will be his
contacts on the outside; relaying to us whatever information he gleans from his investigation.”

Looking at my brothers, I said, “I suppose it’ll make me feel safer; knowing you two are looking out for me.”

The five of us talked for a bit longer until Captain Burnett ended the meeting. Stepping towards the door, Captain Burnett turned to Darren and Andrew, “It’s going to take a few days before Matthew’s ready to go undercover; but I want you both to start getting things ready.” Pausing, he said, “Start by finding him an apartment to live in.”

“Wait; how exactly am I going to even get close to Miguel?”

“That’s where all the intelligence you gathered over the last year will come in handy.” Looking at me, Captain Burnett said, “Once your new identity is finalized, we’ll get you a job at Miguel’s favorite nightclub.”

“Great; so not only will I have to seduce a drug lord’s son, but I’ll also have to deal with other guy’s hitting on me.” Groaning, I said, “This better be worth it.”

Looking at me, Darren said, “Don’t worry brother, we’ll make sure nothing happens to you.”

After Darren and Andrew walked off, Captain Burnett turned to me and Ms. Stewart. “Now Matthew, I know this goes way beyond your job description; but it’s crucial that we take down Hector Chavez and his goons.” Pausing, he said, “I want you to go with Ms. Stewart here. You two will work together to create your undercover identity.”

“Understood, sir.”
1211.title: Learning of My Assignment

12111.content:
Ms. Stewart and I didn’t talk much during the drive to Radeon; save for exchanging idle pleasantries. She presented herself as the no-nonsense type, and I was understandably fixated on the fact that I was about to become a woman.

The local Radeon office was situated in a modest three-story building in an office park. After parking the car, Ms. Stewart and I entered the building through a side door and took an elevator to the second floor. Leading me through a doorway into a small office, Ms. Stewart said, “Please have a seat Mr. Collins; the sooner we start, the sooner you can start your assignment.”

Taking a seat, I said, “I’d appreciate it if you called me Matthew, or Matt.”

“Very well, Matthew.” Sighing as she turned on her computer, Ms. Stewart said, “If you feel like it, you may call me Loren.”

“Okay Loren.”

Judging from the expression on her face, Loren wasn’t pleased that I’d taken her up on her officer. Turning to me, she said, “What do you know about this company and what we do?”

“I know absolutely nothing about this company; I didn’t even know you existed before today.”

“Well then, let me enlighten you.” Taking a breath, Loren said, “This company uses proprietary technology to change everything about a person. We typically do this for psychological research to see how people fair in new situations; but occasionally we partner with local and Federal law enforcement.”

“So, you’re going to inject me with something that’ll turn me into a woman?” chuckling, I said, “I’m sorry; but I still find that hard to believe.”

Retrieving a framed photo from the shelf behind her, Loren placed it on the desk on front of me. “The person in that photo used to be me.”

I looked at the photo and was shocked to see a man, probably no older than 17-years-old, wearing a football uniform. Turning my attention from the photo to Loren, I said, “You’re saying that boy is…was you?” getting nervous, I said, “Forgive me; but I thought you said this was all reversible.”

“Yes, that boy was me. Yes, everything is reversible.” Sighing, Loren said, “I had the misfortune of having a genetic condition that prevented me from changing back.”

Although she clearly had reservations about it, Loren explained how her life as a boy ended. According to her she’d received a football scholarship to the University of Florida; only to have it taken away following an accident that fractured her spine in three places. Still desperate to get a college education, she signed with Radeon for a two-year study.

“The plan was that I’d spend two years as a woman, then they’d give me the money for college.” Taking the photo back, Loren said, “Unfortunately fate had other ideas; so, I was stuck like this. Radeon felt terrible about what happened, so they offered me a job.”

Feeling sorry for her, I said, “Do you parents and friends know what happened to you?”

“Obviously my parents know; they pass me off as a niece.” Sighing, she said, “As for my friends; they think I’m dead. My parents had a funeral a few months back.”

“I’m truly sorry about all that, Loren.”

For the first time since I met her, Loren’s tone of voice was pleasant, “There’s no need for you to apologize; it’s not your fault.” Looking at me, she said, “Would I have liked to be my old self again? Yeah, you bet. But at least this way I have a decent career and a boyfriend who loves me.”

After talking for a few moments, I looked at Loren and said, “We should probably get to work now, don’t you think?” Returning to her no-nonsense attitude, Loren said, “First things first; you need to come up with a name.”

I knew I had to give that some thought, as it would be my identity for a while. After pondering it for a moment, I said, “How about Maria Hernandez.”

“That’s as good a name as any, I suppose.” Typing it into her computer, Loren said, “Okay Maria, what kind of woman do you want to be?”
12111.title: At Radeon

121111.content:
It took Loren and me two hours to develop every aspect of my new identity. I don’t know why, but I didn’t think it would take that long. First came the biographical information. I was a 21-year-old woman from Veracruz; having immigrated to the US when I was 18 years old in search of a better life. I was working towards become a citizen. My family was killed during a drug war in 2017, leaving me orphaned at the age of 17.

As Loren was putting the information into the system, she looked at me and said, “I hope you realize what you’re getting yourself into here. And I’m not talking about going undercover with a cartel.” Pausing, she said, “You’re going to be a 21-year-old Mexican national living in the US; there’s going to be people who assume you’re here illegally.”

“I’ll already be dealing with hardened criminals; racists shouldn’t be a problem.” Sighing, I said, “I’ll just have to be a woman who can take care of herself.”

Once we were finished with that, Loren and I got to work on Maria’s physical attributes. “Since your assignment is to seduce Miguel Chavez, it’s best that we make you into a woman he’d be interested in.” looking at me, Loren said, “What can you tell me about his ideal woman?”

“The guys never had a girlfriend for more than a few months, so I really don’t know.” Leaning back in the chair, I said, “From what I do know; he seems to go for girls that around 5’6” or so. He’s dated well-endowed girls and girls who were flat-chested, so it’s hard to know where he stands on that.”

“Okay then; why don’t we go right down the middle.” Typing away on her computer, Loren said, “We’ll make you into a woman whose about five-eight, with a chest just north of a B-cup.”

I was about to make a comment when Loren suddenly grabbed a camera and took a photo of me. Looking at her as she connected the camera to the computer, I said, “What was that for?”

“The computer needed a photo of you in order to simulate the changes.” After several seconds, she turned the monitor towards me and said, “Why don’t you have a look at the finished product.”

Looking at the monitor, I was floored by what I saw. I watched while my image changed as the computer applied the paraments Loren and I discussed. Once it was finished, my image had been replaced with that of an attractive Mexican woman. “Wow, that’s incredible.”

“If you think that’s incredible, wait until you see the real thing.” Inserting a flash drive into her computer, Loren said, “Now let’s get this to the lab so you can start the procedure.”

******************************


It wasn’t long after that I found myself strapped to an inclined table in a small room, wearing a pair of boxers and a tank top lent to me by the lab tech. Loren was standing nearby while the tech was sticking needles into various parts of my body. Looking at me has he worked, the tech said, “While you’ll be asleep for most of the procedure, you’ll still see the initial changes taking place.”

“That’s cool, I guess.” Curious, I said, “How long will this take?”

Stepping forward, Loren said, “The whole procedure will take an hour or so; after that we’ll move you to a private room to recover.”

As Loren and the tech stepped into a nearby room, I said to myself, “I really hope this is worth it.”

I didn’t know the procedure had started until I felt a tingling sensation in my chest. Craning my neck enough to look down, I watched as a pair of breasts grew on my chest. That was the last thing I saw before losing consciousness.
121111.title: Creating My New Identity

1211111.content:
I became increasingly aware of new sensations as I rejoined the world of the conscious. Chief among them was the added weight on my chest and the void between my legs. Ay dios mio, those were some of my favorite parts. Opening my eyes, I found my vision blocked by a fair amount of brown hair.

Using a hand to move the hair away, I was surprised to see how dainty and feminine my hand had become. Not only that, but it was now darker than it had ever been before – as if I’d spent a few hours in a tanning booth.

Without giving it any thought, I hopped out of bed and entered the nearby bathroom to get a closer look at myself. It hadn’t occurred to me when I saw it on the monitor, but the new me bared a passing resemblance to Michelle Rodriguez. “Oh well; if I’m going to be a woman, I may as well be an attractive one.”

My attention was quickly drawn to my new voice; it was feminine for sure, with a modest accent. My voice kind of reminded me of a girl I had a crush on in Middle School. Turning my attention back to my body, I focused on my chest. Grabbing hold of my new breasts, I said to myself, “I’m going to have some fun with you girls later.”

I wasn’t quite ready to venture down below the waist; but even if I was, I was interrupted by someone knocking on the door in the other room. After a few seconds I heard the door open, followed by Loren’s voice as she said, “Maria? Are you in here?”

I had to remind myself that I was Maria Hernandez now. Stepping out of the bathroom, I saw Loren standing by the door with a tablet in her hands. “What can I do for you, Loren?”

“Wow.” Looking at me, Loren said, “That computer simulation didn’t do you justice; you look even better in person.”

“Thanks, I guess.” Crossing my arms, I said, “I just hope I’m the type of woman that Miguel would be interested in.”

“I’m glad you brought that up; because your assignment is contingent on you being attracted to him as well.” Handing the tablet to me, she said, “There are two images I want you to look at; one is a woman and the other is a man. I need you to tell me which you’re attracted to.”

Taking the tablet, I looked at the first image – Loren posing on a beach in a bikini. Normally I would’ve found her to be incredibly attractive, but now that wasn’t the case at all. Looking at her, I said, “You look great in that bikini; but I’m afraid it does nothing for me.”

“Thanks for the compliment.” Reaching over to swipe the screen to the right, she said, “How about this one?”

Looking down at the screen once again, I saw some random guy posing in a bathing suit. In contrast to Loren’s photo, I found myself aroused by what I was seeing. I could feel my nipples going erect as my heart started racing. Forcibly giving the tablet back to Loren, I said, “Okay; it’s confirmed that I like guys now.”

“That’s good; because it wouldn’t make sense to seduce someone you’re not attracted to.” Turning off the tablet, Loren said, “I’ll have some clothes delivered to you; once you’re dressed, I’ll arrange for you to be brought to a hotel.”

I was about to ask why I was going to a hotel; until I realized it would take some time to find me a place to live in. “I appreciate the help, Loren.” Looking at her, I said, “I know you’re in communication with my Captain; has he given you any updates yet?”

“Your brothers are still working on finding you a place to live.” Looking at me as she turned towards the door, Loren said, “Captain Burnett said he was able to get you a job interview at the nightclub. It sounds like the owner owes him a favor, so you’re guaranteed the job.”
1211111.title: Waking Up

12111111.content:
The clothes that Loren had delivered consisted of a pair of jeans, knee-length boots, and a light green V-neck blouse. There was also some underwear and a bra as well. “Thanks, I guess.” Shuffling my feet in embarrassment, I said, “I suppose there’s one good thing to come from all this; I’ll have firsthand knowledge of what makes women tick.”

As the two of us started walking down the hall, Loren said, “That’s exactly what I thought after I changed; but we both know how that turned out.” Looking at me, her face sorrowful, Loren said “At least you’ll get a chance to put that knowledge to use once your assignments finished.”

I don’t know why, but I felt the need to cheer Loren up. “I think you’re looking at your situation all wrong, Loren.” Placing a hand on her shoulder, I said, “You mentioned having a boyfriend, right?”

“Yeah, he’s name is Harry.” Looking at me, wearily, Loren said, “What’s your point?”

“You have 17 years of experience being a man; you know what they want and how they think.” Stopping in the middle of the hallway, I said, “You’ll never have a girlfriend; but you can still be the best girlfriend in the world.”

“You know, I never thought of it that way.” Her facing perking up, Loren said, “You have a real talent for cheering people up.”

“You forget that I’m an orphan; I’ve had a lot of people cheer me up over the years.”

The two of us continued walking through the halls; eventually reaching the main entrance where I saw a man waiting out front. As we stepped outside, Loren said, “This is Dominic; he’s going to take you to the Azure Crown Hotel.”

Dominic opened the rear passenger door for me; I was about to enter when I decided to talk with Loren one last time. Pulling her to the side, I said, “I’m sure you’ve already thought of this; but what will do about identification and all that?”

“That’s being taken care of; you’ll have everything you need by tomorrow.” Looking at me, she said, “As far as the United States government will be concerned, you’re a Mexican citizen here on a work visa.”

I was impressed by the level of attention Radeon was giving this; they clearly knew what they were doing. Before returning to the car, I gave Loren a quick hug and said, “Thanks for everything, Loren.”

Hugging me back, Loren said, “Stay safe Maria; bring the bastard down for good.”
12111111.title: Leaving Radeon

121111111.content:
Dominic and I didn’t talk at all during the drive from Radeon. Part of me wondered if that was because Radeon employees weren’t the chatty type. So, to pass the time I looked out the window as we drove over the MacArthur Causeway on the way downtown.

I’d only been to the Azure Crown Hotel once, and that was for a retirement party last year. From what little I’d seen I could tell it was an expensive hotel. It was shortly after 3:30 in the afternoon when Dominic came to a stop in front of the hotel’s entrance.

After being helped from the car by Dominic, I stepped inside and made my way to the front desk. I couldn’t help but notice the looks I was getting from some of the people in the lobby. It didn’t take a genius to realize why they were looking at me. I was a young Mexican woman in her 20s; and my outfit didn’t exactly scream that I was wealthy.

Stepping up to the counter, I looked at the woman behind it and said, “Hello, I’m checking in. The name’s Maria Hernandez.”

Typing something into her computer, the woman looked at me and said, “Ah, Ms. Hernandez; we’ve been expecting you.” With her best customer service smile, the woman said, “As requested, we’ve delivered several days’ worth of clothing to your suite.”

I had no idea what she was talking about; that must’ve been something that Radeon had arranged. “Thank you.”

After getting my room keys, I made my way to the elevator and took that to the 6th floor. The hallway was empty except for an employee delivering room service. I flashed a polite smile as I walked past.

Letting myself into my room, I was floored by what I saw. The suite was huge; the sitting area alone was twice the size of my own bedroom. Closing the door, I said to myself, “Wow; Radeon sure knows how to treat their clients.”

********************************


After checking out the rest of the suite, I sat down on the couch and decided to watch some TV. I did that for a little while before looking at the clock – it was almost 5:30. “Darren and Andrew are probably at the bar by now.” With a grin, I said, “Perhaps I should stop by and have a little fun with them.”

Every Friday after work I’d get together with Darren and Andrew at a bar called Harry’s. Never one to buck tradition, I was certain they’d be there tonight regardless of what was going on. Getting up from the couch, I turned off the TV and went into the
bedroom.

I wasn’t exactly comfortable with wearing a revealing outfit so soon; but I really wanted to have some fun with my brothers. After changing into cocktail dress and heels, I grabbed my things before heading for the door. Stepping out of the room, I saw an older couple coming down the hallway. They both looked like they were in their late 50s. I acknowledged them as they walked by; only for the woman to stop and look at me. “Did you have a good night?”

“Huh?” genuinely confused, I said, “I beg your pardon?”

“Don’t play coy with me; we both know there’s only two reasons someone like you gets into a suite like that.” Looking at me with contempt, the woman said, “We both know you’re not a maid.”

It took me a moment to realize what she was getting at. Once I figured it out, I looked at her and said, “I’m not an escort, if that’s what you’re implying.”

“Well then; you must be someone’s trophy wife.”

Getting riled up, I said, “I’ll have you know my employer is paying for this room.”

That was a stretch, I know; but it was the best I could think of. At that moment I decided to walk away before the woman could say anything else. Great, I thought as I waited for the elevator, barely 4 hours into my new life, and I’m already dealing with racists.
121111111.title: At the Hotel

1211111111.content:
Although the bar was only a few blocks from the hotel, I decided it would be easier on my feet if I took a cab. The entire ride, all I could think about was what that woman had said to me. I knew racism like that existed; hell, I’d even gone on calls where people were spouting racist language. Until today I never imagined I’d be placed in a position where I’d be a target.

The cab stopped in front of Harry’s; I paid the driver and gave him a generous tip as I exited. Starting towards the entrance, I spotted Darren’s car parked a short distance up the road. Excellent, I thought as I walked inside, now to have a little fun at their expense.

It was only as I was getting line to go inside that I realized I had a problem – I didn’t have any identification yet. Sure, I was over 21 but the bouncer wouldn’t take my word for it. I considered my options as the line started moving. Getting closer, I could only think of two options: go back to the hotel or show a little skin.

I reached a decision as I stopped in front of the bouncer, “I forgot my ID back at the hotel, but perhaps you could pretend you didn’t see me?” leaning forward, accentuating my breasts, I said, “I can make it worth your while.”

The bouncer clearly wasn’t in the mood for some random girl flirting with him, “Just go inside already.”

I walked into the bar with a smile on my face; wondering what else I could get just by showing a little cleavage. Stopping just inside the bar, I looked around until I found Darren sitting at the bar. If I were going to mess with anyone, it would be him – he fancied himself a lady’s man.

With a grin on my face, I sauntered up to the bar and took a seat next to him. God, I thought, I can’t believe I’m about to flirt with my brother. Taking a moment to gather my courage, I turned to him and said, “Hey there.”

“Hey there yourself.” Giving me a once over, Darren said, “What’s a beautiful woman like you doing here by yourself?”

I never understood why Darren always used those cliched pickup lines; until I remembered he rarely left the bar without some girl by his side. With a fake smile, I said, “I’m here on vacation; I heard some guys talking about this place and thought I’d check it out.” Rubbing my hand against his thigh, I said, “Perhaps we could get to know each other over a drink?”

Darren quickly obliged by ordering me a strawberry daiquiri – I would’ve ordered something stronger, but I didn’t know what my tolerance was in this body. After that, the two of us adjourned to a nearby booth to talk. As we sat down, I started to wonder where Andrew was. Suddenly, as if he read my mind, Darren said, “My brother’s meeting me here shortly; I hope you don’t mind the company.”

“Of course not; the more the merrier.” Trying my best to be seductive, I said, “Why don’t you tell me about yourself?”

I pretended to pay attention for the next 20 minutes as Darren told me about himself – I, of course, already knew everything about him. I told him about myself as well; all while I was doing my best to flirt with him.

There was one thing I noticed as time went by; the longer I talked with him, the less I had to pretend I was flirting with him. Never in my wildest dreams did I ever think I’d be in a position where I’d be flirting with my brother – but then I never imagined I’d be turned into a woman.

I was well into my second drink when Andrew finally showed up. Introductions were made as he joined us in the booth. “Well man; it looks like you won’t be going home alone tonight.” Eying me, Andrew said, “She’s definitely a looker.”

Up until now I’d only been pretending to flirt with Darren; it never occurred to me that he’d try to take me home with him. I decided right then that I was going to put a stop to my charade. But before I could say anything, some random drunk bumped the table and spilled my drink all over my outfit.
1211111111.title: At the Bar

12111111111.content:
I was too stunned to say anything as what was left of my drink seeped through my outfit. For a fleeting moment I was more concerned about getting the stain out than anything else. I made a feeble attempt to soak up the spill, but it was useless.

Giving up on the dress, I focused my attention on what was going on in front of me. Darren and Andrew were on their feet as soon as the guy bumped our table. I begrudgingly accepted the man’s apology, if only to rid myself of his godawful stench. Watching his stumble off, I thought to myself, This isn’t how I thought today was going to go. In barely two hours of living as a woman, I’d been accused of being a prostitute and had some drunk spill a drink on me. I’ll never look at woman the same way again.

After the man walked off, Darren and Andrew returned to their seats. “I’m sorry about that; they really should cut people off if they’re acting like that.” Eying my stained dress, Darren said, “Will you be able to save that dress?”

“I don’t know. My hotel has a laundry service, I’ll see what they can do.” Getting to my feet, I said, “I should probably take this as a sign to call it a night.”

“Come on; you can’t let some drunk ruin a fun time.” Stepping in front of me, Darren said, “Why don’t you stay a little longer; then we can go back to my place and see where the evening takes us.”

For a fleeting second, I actually found myself considering Darren’s offer; until I remembered who he was, and my relationship to him. “Unfortunately, I think I’m going to pass.” Looking at him, trying my best to act disappointed, I said, “You’re a really sweet guy, but I’m not I want to spend the night with you.”

Desperate to salvage the evening, Darren said, “So what’s your name?”

“It’s nice to meet you, Maria.” Smiling, Darren said, “Now can we spend the night together?”

12111111111.title: Continue

121111111111.content:
Going into this, I wasn’t sure how either of them would react. Neither of them said anything for about a minute as their mouths opened and closed. After a few minutes of this, Darren looked at me and said, “M-Matt?”

1211111111111.title: During the Night

12111111111111.title: The Next Morning

121111111111111.content:

Speaking of Darren, I couldn’t help but notice he was fixated on me. I didn’t mind it at first, but it started getting annoying after a while.

Things came to a head as I carried my empty dishes into the kitchen. I hadn’t gone five feet before I heard Captain Burnett yell, “Detective Acton!”

Startled, I spun around in time to see Darren quickly look away. It didn’t take a genius to figure out what he’d been doing. Approaching him, I said, “Were you staring at me as I walked away?”

“No, of course not.”

“You can’t fool me, Darren; I know when you’re lying.” Glaring at him, I said, “I’d appreciate it if you stopped staring at me like that.
I know I don’t look it, but I’m still your brother.”

Looking down in shame, Darren said, “Sorry Matt.”

I was about to walk away, when I stopped and said, “That’s another thing; stop calling me Matt. My name’s Maria Hernandez.”

Reading the room, Captain Burnett said, “Perhaps now is an appropriate time for us to get going.” Getting up from the chair, he approached me and said, “Don’t forget you have that interview is in several hours. It’s only a formality; the owner owes me a favor.”

After saying goodbye, I saw the others out before adjourning to the bedroom. Thankfully, I had time before the interview because I had absolutely no clue how to do hair and makeup.
121111111111111.title: Visitors

1211111111111111.content:
Thanks to that coked up burglar, my parents never got the chance to teach me a lot of life lessons. One of the few I remembered was that I should always dress for my dream job. That was a lesson I’d always taken to heart. And even though I was interviewing for a job as a cocktail waitress at a nightclub, I still felt the need to dress professionally.

What did that entail, you ask? It entailed a beige skirt suit and heels. Once I was satisfied with my outfit, I went to the bathroom to work on my hair and makeup. Let me tell you – the wide array of makeup tutorials on YouTube were a godsend. While I’m sure a natural born woman would find something to critique; I thought I did a decent job on my first attempt.

Before leaving the room, I took the opportunity to call downstairs and request a cab – lamenting the fact that my new identity didn’t come with a valid drivers license. Making sure to grab my purse, I opened the door and stepped out – only to spot the woman from last evening walking down the hall.

Seeing the contemptuous look on her face, I knew she was going to say something. Sure enough, she stopped in front of me and said, “I think I’ve finally figured out how you can afford such an expensive room.”

“Oh.” Crossing my arms, I said, “How’s that?”

“You’re nothing more than a high-priced escort.” With a smug look on her face, she continued, “Judging by how quickly that young man left last night; I can only imagine how expensive you are.”

I was getting angry at this point, “I don’t know what your issue is with me, ma’am; and frankly I don’t care.” Stepping past her, I continued, “Perhaps you’d fine your time better spent finding someone in this hotel who’d give a rats ass about what you think of me.”

The woman was speechless as I walked down the hall; while I was barely able to contain the utter joy that I was feeling.
1211111111111111.title: Leaving for the Interview

12111111111111111.content:
Paradise was relatively new to the Miami club scene; having only opened 2 years ago. It was about a block or two away from the beach, and about a 15-minute taxi ride from the hotel. After paying the cab fare, I stepped out and looked at the building’s façade.

My parents had always hoped I’d become some successful businessman like my Dad. As I approached the front door, I wondered how they would feel knowing I was about to interview for a job as a cocktail waitress.

I’d spoken to Captain Burnett during the cab ride, so I knew the owner was expecting me. He must’ve been waiting at the door because I didn’t even get a chance to knock. Until now I’d wondered why a nightclub owner would owe Captain Burnett a favor.
The moment I caught a glimpse of the owner, I found out my answer – he was Captain Burnett’s brother.

Looking at me, he said, “You must be Detective Hernandez.”

It seemed Captain Burnett hadn’t been entirely open with his brother; and, for safety reasons, I decided that I wasn’t going to be either. If he knew me as Detective Hernandez that’s who I was going to be. Offering my hand, I said, “That’s right; I’m Detective Maria Hernandez.”

“It’s nice to meet you.” Taking my hand, he said, “My name is Duncan Burnett; you probably realized I’m your boss’s younger brother.”

Duncan stepped aside to let me in; as I did, I said, “The resemblance is rather striker; although you’re much handsomer than he is.”

That wasn’t me playing nice; Duncan was much easier on the eyes then Captain Burnett. I had to reel myself in once I realized I was fawning over my boss’s brother. Closing the front door, Duncan said, “My brother didn’t really go into detail about what you’re doing here, so perhaps you could fill me in?”

Following after Duncan as he walked through the club, I said, “I’m afraid most of that is need to know; but what I can tell you is I’m hoping to infiltrate the Chaves cartel. Hector’s son Miguel is a regular patron of your club, so I’m going to try and seduce him.”

Gesturing me into his office, Duncan said, “You may discover that to be particularly challenging; he’s always surrounded by women of a promiscuous nature.” Taking a seat at his desk, he continued, “I’ve only just met you; but I suspect you’re not that type of woman.”

Sitting across from Duncan, I said, “There’s more to me than meets the eye.”

“Very well then; don’t say I didn’t warn you.” Taking a breath as he turned on his computer, Duncan said, “Miguel typically comes by on Sunday evenings. Now I do have a hole in the schedule for tonight, one of my girls twisted her ankle bad last night. It’s up to you, but I would suggest you work tonight to get the lay of the land.”

After giving it a few seconds thought, I said, “That sounds like a reasonable suggestion; what time should I be here?”

“We open at 9PM; so why don’t you be here for 8PM.” Looking at me, he said, “I’ll introduce you to the staff and you can get into uniform.”

For the next few minutes Duncan and I hammered out the details of my employment. Once that was finished, he escorted me back to the front of the building. After thanking him for the job, I said, “I wonder how people would feel; knowing the Head of the Narcotics Unit has a brother who runs a nightclub.”

“They’d be surprised for sure; but Howard and I don’t talk business when we’re together.” Opening the door, he said, “I’ll see you tonight, Detective.”

Stepping out onto the sidewalk, I was about to call for a cab when I heard a familiar voice, “Need a lift?”

Turning to my left, I saw Darren leaning against his car a short distance away. Walking towards him, I said, “What are you doing here, Darren?” getting agitated, I said, “I’m supposed to be undercover; what if someone from the Cartel sees me with you.”

“Relax; don’t get your panties in a bunch.” Opening the passenger door, he said, “I happened to be in the area and thought you’d like some lunch.”

I wasn’t buying Darren’s explanation for a second, but I was hungry. “Fine.” Getting in the car, I said, “A quick lunch and then you’ll bring me back to the hotel.”
12111111111111111.title: The Interview

121111111111111111.content:
Darren brought to me a café overlooking the water. He ordered his typical cholesterol-laden meal, a burger and fries. I normally would have ordered something similar but now I had a figure I needed to watch. So I instead ordered a Caesar salad and a diet coke.

That sudden change in my character was enough for Darren to say, “You're not going native, are you? First there’s your outfit, now you’re eating something that’s actually healthy for you?”

“Since when is eating healthy something only women do?” Raising an eyebrow, I said, “I’ve been wanting to eat better for a while; suddenly having a figure to maintain seemed like a good reason to start.”

“I’ll give you that one.” Looking at me as the server delivered our drinks before walking off, Darren said, “But what about everything else. I’m sorry, but you actually look comfortable in that outfit.”

I’d be lying if I said the skirt wasn’t comfortable; it was oddly freeing to let that area breathe. I never realized just how constricting pants could be. That being said, I wasn’t about to admit that to Darren, “I wasn’t going to show up for interview wearing shorts and a t-shirt.”

“Come on, we both know the interview was a formality. You could have shown up in a two-piece and gotten the job.” Taking a sip of his soda, he said, “I must say though; that skirt does an amazing job showing off your figure.”

I had planned to take a sip of my own soda until Darren said that. Instead I put it down and looked at him, “I’d really appreciate it if you stopped making comments like that.” Sighing, I continued, “Like I told you this morning; I may not look it, but I’m still your brother.”

I was extremely grateful that no one was nearby to hear me say that. “Yeah, right.” Scoffing at me, Darren said, “If you’re really still my brother, then what was with that kiss last night?”
I could feel myself blushing, “That kiss meant nothing, Darren; I’d had 2 drinks and was feeling things I never felt before.” Sighing, I said, “That woman I told you and Andy about; she was walking down the hall. I thought the kiss would prove her wrong.”

“Let me get this straight; that woman thought you were an escort. In order to prove her wrong, you decided to kiss me?” shaking his head, Darren said, “That makes absolutely no sense; even if it did, it wouldn’t explain the passion that was in that kiss.”

Blushing even more, I said, “I don’t know what you’re talking about.”

“The hell you don’t; you enjoyed that kiss.” Looking at me, Darren said, “If I’m being honest here, so did I.”

Darren was right; on some level that was the best kiss I’d ever had. Part of me enjoyed the fact that he felt the same. But I wasn’t about to admit that to him. Thankfully I was saved by the server bringing our food.

“I’d appreciate it if we stopped talking about the kiss; it never should have happened in the first place.”

**********


After Darren and I finished lunch, the two of us walked back to the car. As I climbed in the passenger seat, he said, “Should I bring you back to the hotel now?”

“No, not yet.” Looking at Darren as I buckled my seat belt, I said, “Can you drive me to Walmart real quick? I need to get some things I’m not comfortable asking the hotel for.”

“Okay, no problem.” Pulling into traffic, Darren said, “What exactly do you need to buy?”

I’d hoped Darren would get the hint that I didn’t want him to know either, but that was asking too much. “Duncan told me that Miguel is regularly in the company of promiscuous women; if I’m going to seduce him, I need to make myself stand out.” Pausing, I said, “To that end, I’m going to buy some push-up bras to enhance my chest.”

Raising an eyebrow, Darren said, “What’s wrong with the hotel knowing about that?”

“That’s not what I was concerned about; I didn’t want them getting me feminine hygiene products.”

“Feminine hygiene products?” realizing what I was getting at, he said, “You can’t be serious.”

“I’m a woman, Darren; as strange as it is for me to say, I will have periods while undercover.”
121111111111111111.title: Lunch

choices: My First Shift at the Club
1211111111111111111.content:
We’ve been at Walmart for almost an hour now; and I was thoroughly enjoying torturing Darren. I talked him into pretending to be my boyfriend as we strolled through the store.

That entailed grabbing various items of clothing and trying them on – things like short skirts and even a bikini. I will admit to enjoying the looks I was getting from Darren.

At present I was in the changing room trying on an evening gown. Stepping outside, I walked over to where Darren was patiently waiting with the shopping cart. Doing a quick spin, I said, “What do you think, babe?”

The look Darren gave me; if looks could kill, I’d be dead. “You told me you only needed a few things; you’ve spent the last 30 minutes trying on clothes.”

“What can I say, I caught the shopping bug. You can’t blame me though, Radeon only gave me a weeks worth of clothes.” Looking at him, I said, “Now seriously, how I do I look?”

After waiting a beat, Darren said, “You look great, that color brings out your eyes. Can we please go now?”

Having tormented Darren enough, I said, “Let me get changed; then we can buy the feminine products on the way out.”

Returning to the dressing room, I closed the door and looked at myself in the mirror. In less than 12 hours I’d not only flirted with my brother at a bar; but I’d also kissed him. Now I was asking his opinion of my outfits. Is something wrong with me?
1211111111111111111.title: Shopping

12111111111111111111.content:
I took a nap after getting back from my lunch & shopping date with Darren; it turns out shopping can really tire a girl out. It was 6:00 once I finally woke up; I hopped into the shower after ordering a sandwich from room service. Once that was done, I put together the skimpiest outfit I could from the clothes I had available.

It was 7:15 when I went downstairs to hail a cab. I was standing just outside the lobby when I heard the honk of a car’s horn. Half expecting to see Darren, I was surprised when I saw Duncan pull up next to me. Lowering the passenger window, he said “I hope you don’t mind; but I thought I’d save you a few bucks.”

Climbing in the front seat, I said, “Thanks. This certainly beats having some taxi driver ogling me.”

“You’ll get plenty of that tonight, believe me.” Glancing at me as he left the hotel, Duncan said, “I must say; I’ve never seen a detective dress quite like that before.”

“Believe me; this is the first time I’ve ever dressed like this.” With a sigh, I said, “I just hope Miguel takes the bait; or else everything I’ve gone through will have been for nothing.”
12111111111111111111.title: Getting Ready for Work

121111111111111111111.content:
It was just before 8:30 when I left the ladies room; having gone in there twice in the last 15 minutes to adjust my uniform in private. I realized early on that I didn’t need the push-up bra; as the outfit I was wearing already did a decent job enhancing my chest.

There was a mirror on the wall next to where the waitresses clocked in. I was looking at reflection in the mirror, once again wondering what I’d gotten myself into, when I noticed another girl approach me.

She was an attractive girl; not really my type but I’d give her a chance. “You’re the new girl, right?” With an outreached hand, she said, “My name’s Isabel, but everyone just calls me Izzy.”

“It’s nice to meet you, Izzy; my name’s Maria.” Looking at her, I said, “I have to admit, I’m feeling pretty nervous about going out there.”

“I can’t say I blame you, since I felt the same way on my first day.” With a smile, Izzy said, “Would you like a piece of friendly advice?”

“Sure.”

“You’re going to have a lot of guys hitting on you; especially since you’re the new girl. Duncan’s a nice guy, but he doesn’t get involved unless things turn serious.” Placing a hand on my shoulder, Izzy continued, “But there’s a silver lining, so don’t fret. By the time they’re drunk enough to hit on you, they’re usually so hammered that you’ll have no problem avoiding them.”

I was touched by Izzy’s concern; I’d only known her for a few minutes, but I could tell she was a genuinely nice person. “Thanks for the advice, Izzy.” Looking at her, I continued, “I hope you don’t take this the wrong way or anything; but you don’t seem like the type of girl who works at a place like this.”

“I don’t have any self-esteem issues, if that’s what you mean.” Chuckling at she fixed her hair in the mirror, Izzy continued, “I’m just here to pay the bills while I go to school; I’m studying to be a dental technician. What about you?”

I knew Izzy wouldn’t believe me if I told her the truth; add to that the fact it would blow my cover. “I’m saving money so I can become a US citizen.”

“That’s a worthy goal, I wish you luck.” Checking the clock behind me, Izzy said, “We’re about to open; let’s get out front.”
121111111111111111111.title: Starting My First Shift

1211111111111111111111.content:
The club’s been open for almost two hours by now; and I’d been on my feet pretty much the entire time. I’ve been to a few nightclubs as a guest before, so I thought I knew what to expect. It turns out that the nightclub experience is radically different for the servers. I lost count of how many guys would either slap my ass or call me a sexist name.

It eventually got the point where I needed to take a break; so, after making one last round through my section, I put down my tray and went to sit in the back. The moment I sat down, I looked up at the ceiling and said, “Dios mio, what did I get myself into?”

“You’ll get used to it after a few days; you just need time.”

Startled, I looked around to find Izzy standing just outside the employee bathroom. “How long did it take you to get used to it?”

“It took me a little longer than most; probably a week or two.” Sitting on the bench next to me, Izzy said, “But then I reminded myself why I was doing this.”

“Don’t you ever feel like people are judging you solely because you’re working here?”

“I’d be lying if I said I didn’t; but then I remember that every leer and sexist remark is another step towards my goal.” Getting to her feet, Izzy said, “Let me give you one piece of advice, something I had to learn the hard way. Be careful what you say around the other girls; they love to pry and do whatever they can in order to get ahead.”

I like to think I’ve develop a talent for reading people; something that comes in handy in my line of work. With that being said, I honestly couldn’t tell if Izzy was someone I could trust. She seemed nice enough but I couldn’t shake the feeling that she was hiding something. “You’re not one of those girls, are you?”

Standing in front of me, Izzy said, “That’s just it; you can never really know for sure.”

With that, Izzy and I went out front to continue our shift.

*************


Early the next morning, after the club closed, I was standing in back with the rest of the girls. We were waiting for Duncan to give us our share of the tips.

While we waited I tried to make conversation with a few of the dancers, but that didn’t go over to well. Apparently the dancers didn’t exactly like the servers.

After getting my share of the tips, about $300, I clocked out and went out the back. I was about to walk up front to hail a cab when Izzy came up next to me, “Looks like you survived your first shift.” Looking at me with a smile on her face, Izzy said, “You need a lift back to your place?”

“I wouldn’t want to inconvenience you, it’s kind of far.”

“Nonsense.” Grabbing me by the arm, Izzy said, “I’m driving you home; just tell me where.”
1211111111111111111111.title: During the Shift

12111111111111111111111.content:
I suppose if there was one positive thing to come from my current situation, it was the fact that I could sleep in as late I wanted. Rolling over in bed, I saw that it was almost 11:00 and figured it was time to get out of bed.

Retrieving my cellphone while on the way to the bathroom, I saw several texts – one from Duncan telling me I had to work tonight, and one from Darren saying I needed to stop by the station. I was about to call Darren about that text when I saw him calling me. Answering the phone, I said, “You must’ve read my mind; I was just about to call you.” Closing the bathroom door, I said, “Why do I need to stop by the Station?”

“I don’t know all the details; but somehow the DEA caught wind of your assignment. It seems that they’ve got an undercover agent of their own.” Pausing, Darren said, “Sounds like they want to pool resources.”

I felt uneasy as I leaned against the bathroom counter, “That means telling them who I really am, doesn’t it?”

“Andrew and I had the same concerns; but we were promised that information would only come out if you were okay with it.” Sighing, Darren said, “But if I were you, I’d consider coming clean; especially in the spirit of cooperation.”

“When you put it that way, I suppose it wouldn’t hurt.” Running a hand through my hair, I said, “Would you mind coming to pick me up in like 35 minutes?”

“Of course, I wouldn’t mind. I’ll see you soon.”

After hanging up the phone, I stripped out of my pajamas and hopped in the shower.

****************************


It was just before Noon when Darren and I walked up the steps leading to the Station. I could see Andrew walking out to meet us. I noticed that he was focusing on the outfit I was wearing. In contrast to the skirt suit I wore yesterday, I decided to wear a pants suit instead. Stopping in front of Andrew, I hugged him and said, “Don’t worry, Andy; it’s still me under all this.”

Giving me an awkward hug, Andy said “If you say so. I just hope you’ll be your old self once this is all finished.”

Following Darren and Andrew inside, I said, “Believe me; no one wants that more than I do.”

As the three of us stepped inside the elevator, Darren turned to Andrew, “Have our DEA guests arrived yet?”

“Yeah, they’re in the conference room with Captain Burnett.” Blushing, Andrew said, “Just wait until you see their undercover operative; she’s a real knockout.”

Stepping out of the elevator, I couldn’t resist saying, “What a wonderful time for me to be a straight woman.”

That elicited a laugh from all three of us as we walked down the hallway. Being the gentleman he was, Darren opened the door to let me inside. I stopped in my tracks the instant I caught sight of the DEA’s agent. “Izzy?”
12111111111111111111111.title: The Next Day

121111111111111111111111.content:
A far cry from what she looked like at the club, Izzy was now wearing a similar outfit to mine. “Well, this wasn’t what I expected.” Looking at me, Izzy said, “Hello Maria.”

Darren was confused, “The two of you know each other?”

“Yeah; we’re both waitresses at the club.” Turning my attention to Izzy, I said, “So you’re the undercover Fed I’m supposed to be working with?”

“I’ve seen firsthand what drugs can do to people; I’ve made it my life’s work to protect as many as I can.”

“I can certainly sympathize with that; I lost my parents to a burglar who was high on god knows what.” Taking a seat at the table, I said, “That’s why I became a cop; to avenge their death and keep others from experiencing the same thing.”

“Hector Chavez is my white whale; I’ve spent every day since leaving the academy trying to bring him down.” Looking at me, anger in her voice, she said, “There’s nothing I won’t do to put that scum behind bars, and see his cartel shut down for good.”

Once again I realized Izzy and I had a lot in common, “It’s nice to know we’re all working towards the same goal here.”

“I’m open to working with Miami PD to bring down Chaves; but there’s only one problem.” Focusing on me, she said, “There’s no Maria Hernandez, or anyone who looks like you, on the department payroll. If we’re going to be working together, you better tell me who you really are.”

I sighed, knowing my hands were tied, “The reason you can’t find anything on Maria Hernandez is because she didn’t exist until a few days ago.” Taking a deep breath, I said, “My real name is Matthew Collins.”

Izzy said nothing for a moment, until, “You’re pulling my leg.”

“Trust me; I’m as determined as you are to bring down Chavez and his cartel. Maybe even more, considering what I had to give up.”

"You don't look like a Matthew Collins."

"That's thanks to an outfit called Radeon Scientific. The Department partnered with them for this assignment; they put me under and the next thing in know, I look like this.” Pausing, I said, “My assignment is to get involved with Chavez’s son Miguel, so I have to look the part.”

"Now, let me get this straight," Izzy said. "You're Matthew Collins, but you let a place called Radeon Scientific turn you into a woman."

"It's the truth, I swear."

I was certain she was going to ask me if I was crazy, or if I thought she was stupid.

Instead, she said, “Wow, so they got you too, huh?”
121111111111111111111111.title: Meeting with the DEA

choices: Later On (Returning to the Conference Room)
1211111111111111111111111.content:
Once all the formalities were out of the way, Izzy and I expected the meeting would get started. That was, however, until both our bosses turned to us, “We’re just going to be discussing the terms of our partnership. Why don’t you ladies go find somewhere to talk.”

Izzy and I both thought it was a wise idea, so we got up from the conference table and left the room. Captain Burnett’s office was just down the hall, so I led Izzy inside. As we both took a seat on the couch, Izzy looked at me and said, “You know, it all makes sense now. Why you were so uncomfortable having drunks flirting with you.”

“I think everyone would be uncomfortable in that position at first; regardless of whether they were born a woman or turned into one like us.”

With a chuckle, Izzy said, “I suppose you’re right about that.” Crossing her legs as she turned to look at me, Izzy said, “That being said, I’m curious to hear how you’re adjusting to womanhood.”

“This may sound strange; but it hasn’t been as hard as I thought it would be. I know it’s only been a few days; but I thought there’d be a learning curve.” Leaning against the back of the couch, I said, “I suppose the biggest thing I’ve had to deal with is the looks I’m getting from my brothers.”

“They’re probably shocked to see their brother is suddenly a woman.” Pausing, Izzy said, “Give it time, I’m sure they’ll come around.”

“It’s more than that, I’m afraid; I’m beginning to suspect that Darren’s attracted to the new me. And if I’m being honest, I think I’m attracted to him as well.”

“Okay wow, that is the last thing I expected to hear.” Slapping her legs with her hands, Izzy said, “Perhaps you two need to sit down and talk this over; because I’m pretty sure incest is illegal in Florida.”

I was confused for a moment, wondering what Izzy was talking about. Then it hit me, “First of all that’s disgusting. Second, Darren’s not my actual brother; he, Andrew and I were in the foster system together. We’re brothers but not in the biological sense.”

Izzy looked at me as she breathed a sigh of relief, “That’s a relief; because I was starting to reconsider teaming up with you.” pausing, she said, “But seriously; if you think there might be something there, you should definitely talk with Darren.”

“That’s not a conversation I’m looking forward to having; especially after what happened my first night as a woman.” Seeing the inquisitive look on Izzy’s face, I said, “Darren brought me back to my hotel; long story short, I kissed him in the hall outside my room.”

“You’re just full of surprises, aren’t you?” grasping my hand in support, Izzy said, “Now I know you should talk to him; specially to clear up any confusion he might have.”

“Thanks.” Sighing, I said, “It’s just weird; finding myself suddenly attracted to guys.”

“If it helps, I know what you’re going through. I freaked out when it happened to me.”

Not fully grasping what Izzy was saying, I replied, “There’s a difference; I wasn’t a lesbian.”

“Neither was I.”
1211111111111111111111111.title: Alone with Izzy

12111111111111111111111111.content:
It took me a second to realize what Izzy was telling me, “You used to be a guy?”

“That’s right; I used to be guy just like you, I went to Radeon just over two years ago.” Turning away briefly, Izzy said, “My original name was Isiah Becker.”

My mind was abuzz with all sorts of questions I wanted to ask. “Why did you turn yourself into a woman?”

“For the same reason you did; to infiltrate the cartel and bring it down from the inside.” After pausing to take a breath, Izzy continued, “But whereas your assignment is to seduce Miguel Chavez; mine is to convince some low-level peon to turn on the cartel.”

“If you’ve been undercover for two years, I imagine you must be having some difficulty.”

“That’s putting it mildly.” Getting frustrated, Izzy explained, “I came close a few months ago; until the idiot I was sleeping with got himself killed by a rival gang.”

“After two years I imagine you can’t wait to be a guy again; picking up girls at bars instead of the other way around.” Leaning back as I looked at Izzy, I said, “I know I’m looking forward to it; and I’ve only been a woman for two days.”

“Yeah, that’s just it.” Taking a deep breath, Izzy said, “I’m not changing back once the assignment is over.”

“Why not? Did you go native or something?”

“If I did go native, it certainly wasn’t by choice.” Staring up at the ceiling, Izzy continued, “I won’t bore you with the details; but seducing low-level drug dealers wasn’t my original assignment.”

My interest was piqued at that point, “Come on, you can’t leave me hanging like this.”

“My original assignment was to pretend I was a crooked DEA agent; work my way up through the ranks until I could discover the cartel’s plans.” Sighing, she said, “That all changed when I encountered this random guy at a bar. Being attracted to men was never part of the arrangement, so you can imagine how I felt when I realized I was attracted to the guy.”

“I don’t see what the big issue is; you won’t be interested in guys once you change back.”

“That’s the problem right there, Maria; I was never supposed to be attracted to them in the first place.” Looking at me, she said, “How can I trust them to return me back to normal after something like that?”

I was starting to understand what Izzy was getting at, “When did you finally decide you weren’t going to change back?”

“About 10 months ago; I decided to accept that I was a heterosexual woman and move on.” Pausing, she said, “A few weeks later, one of my co-workers introduced me to her brother; and we’ve been dating ever since.”

***********************


Izzy and I talked for a little while before deciding it was time to rejoin the others. As we stepped out of the office, Izzy stopped me and said, “I overheard your Captain mention something about finding you a place to live.” Looking at me, she said, “I know we just met; but I have a spare room in my apartment that you’re welcome to have.”

After giving it a few seconds thought, I said, “Sure, why not.”
12111111111111111111111111.title: Continue

121111111111111111111111111.content:
The rest of the meeting went pretty much as I expected. We exchanged whatever information we’d gathered and decided that Miami PD would take the lead in the joint operation. Izzy informed Captain Burnett of her offer to let me live with her; an offer which he was quick to accept.

Once the meeting wrapped up, I escorted Izzy and her associates out of the building. Handing me a piece of paper, she said, “I wrote down my address and phone number; contact me whenever you’re ready to move in.”

I was thanking her once again, when I noticed Darren approaching us with a peculiar look on his face. Turning to face him, her arms crossed, Izzy said, “Can I help you with something, Detective?”

“No; I’m just trying to figure out why you were both willing to become woman for this assignment.” Shaking his head in disbelief, he continued, “I’m sorry; but I can’t help but question your manhood.”

I was stunned by Darren’s assertion; but Izzy beat me to the punch, “Well Detective; perhaps we’re so secure in our manhood that being woman for a while won’t do us any harm.” Gesturing towards me, she said, “Based on what I’ve seen so far, I can bet Maria was more of a man then you’ll ever be.”

Darren looked as though he’d just been kicked in the nuts, his jaw agape. Without saying another word, he turned and walked back into the building. Once he was gone, I turned to Izzy and said, “You left out the part where you’re stuck like this.”

Waving her hand in the air, Izzy said, “He doesn’t need to know that little tidbit.” Starting down the steps, Izzy said, “I’m meeting my boyfriend for lunch; you’re welcome to join if you’d like.”

Smiling as I followed her down the steps, I said, “Sure, why not.”
121111111111111111111111111.title: Leaving the Meeting

1211111111111111111111111111.content:
Izzy and I arrived at a small sandwich shop a few minutes after leaving the station. As the two of us left her car, I said, “Just so I know everything going in; does your boyfriend know about your past?”

Nodding as she opened the door, Izzy said, “Harry knows everything about me; and he’s been very supportive.”

I followed Izzy through the restaurant to a small booth in the back. I watched a tall man get up from his seat and approach Izzy. Giving her a quick kiss, he said, “Who’s your friend, Iz?”

Turning towards me, Izzy said, “Harry Dalton; this is Maria Hernandez, she just started at the nightclub.”

“Really?” reaching over to shake my hand, Harry said, “You certainly don’t look like someone who works at a nightclub.”

“I’m sorry if I don’t conform to your idea of what a waitress at a club looks like.” a little miffed by that remark, I said, “Just because I dress that way for my job doesn’t mean I dress that way all the time. Also need I remind you that your girlfriend also works at the club.”

Harry stepped back when he saw the looks that he was getting from me and Izzy, “You’re right, I shouldn’t have prejudged you.” Reaching out once more to shake my hand, Harry said, “I’m sure you have your reasons for working at the club, and I won’t hold that against you.”

“I’m sorry for snapping at you like that; I’ve had a stressful couple of days.” Shaking Harry’s hand as I sat down, I said, “Why don’t you tell me about yourself, Harry?”

“Really not much to tell; my job isn’t as glamorous as what you ladies do.” With a chuckle, Harry continued, “I’m a dentist; hoping to start my own practice soon.”

“Ugh, I hate the dentist.” Smiling, I said, “Although I must say, you’re much better looking than my last one.”

Harry’s phone rang started ringing right at that moment. Digging it from his pocket, he took one look at it and said, “Sorry ladies, but I have to take this.”

Izzy waited until Harry disappeared around the corner before kicking me in the shin with her heels. Yelping in pain, I reached down to rub my ankle, “What was that for?”

“Look; I know you’re just having fun but be careful. It’s easy to get carried away.” looking at me with a serious look on her face, Izzy continued, “Also Harry’s off limits, so don’t let me catch you flirting with him again.”

“Understood; I won’t flirt with Harry anymore.” With a smile, I said, “But between you and me, Harry is really cute.”

With a smile of her own, Izzy said, “I have great taste in guys, don’t I?”

I was about to say something when I saw Harry returning; the look on his face telling me the call didn’t go well. “I’m sorry ladies but I was called in for an emergency root canal.” Leaning over to kiss Izzy, he said, “I’ll make it up to you, I promise.”

“I’ll be holding you to that promise.”

Harry grabbed his jacket as he looked at me, “It was nice to meet you, Maria.”

“It was nice meeting you as well, Harry.”
1211111111111111111111111111.title: Lunch Time

•Darren Acton & Andrew Pierce: 22 years old; Matthew’s foster brothers. Fellow cops on Narcotics unit.
•Miguel Chavez: 23 years old; son of a drug kingpin. Target of Matthew’s undercover assignment.
•Hector Chavez: 44 years old; drug kingpin.
•Captain Howard Burnett: head of the Narcotics Division

info.brief_description: What if a company had the ability to change everything about a person?
info.description:
Radeon Scientific has the ability to change everything about a person - both mentally and physically. What possible stories could come of this?

Rules:
1. Use proper grammar
2. No descriptive sex scenes (keep it PG)
3. Be creative with storylines but keep close to the original idea.
4. Use the 1st person perspective (I, I'm, I'll, etc) - keep it from the main character's POV

There will be an outline of the characters.
